boogie/stores/graphstore

        traverseBfs

        upsertRelationship
      • upsertRelationship(tx: var GraphTx; r: Relationship)

This module implements a simple graph store on top of the Boogie WAL for durability. The graph store supports basic operations for creating, updating, and deleting nodes and relationships, as well as simple traversal and querying capabilities. The graph data is stored in-memory for fast access, with periodic snapshots to disk for durability. The WAL is used to log all changes to the graph, allowing for crash recovery and durability guarantees.

The main types are:

  • GraphNode: represents a graph node with an ID, labels, and properties
  • Relationship: represents a directed relationship between two nodes, with an ID, type, and properties
  • GraphStore: the main graph store object that manages the in-memory graph and interacts with the WAL

The main operations supported by the graph store include:

  • Creating, updating, and deleting nodes and relationships
  • Querying nodes by label and traversing relationships

Imports

../wal

Types

GraphError = object of CatchableError
GraphNode = object
  id*: uint64
  labels*: seq[string]
  props*: JsonNode
GraphStore {.acyclic.} = ref object
  wal*: Wal
GraphTx = object
Relationship = object
  id*: uint64
  fromId*: uint64
  toId*: uint64
  relType*: string
  props*: JsonNode

Procs

proc beginTx(s: GraphStore): GraphTx {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc closeGraphStore(s: GraphStore) {....raises: [IOError, Exception, OSError,
    WalError], tags: [RootEffect, WriteIOEffect, ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
                                      forbids: [].}
proc commit(tx: var GraphTx) {....raises: [GraphError, IOError, WalError, KeyError,
                                        GraphError, Exception, OSError], tags: [
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect, RootEffect, ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect],
                               forbids: [].}
Commits the transaction, applying all operations atomically to the graph store. The operations are first appended to the WAL for durability, then applied to the in-memory graph state. Finally, a checkpoint is performed to save the current state and reset the WAL. After commit, the transaction is marked as finished and cannot be used for further operations. 
proc createNode(tx: var GraphTx; labels: seq[string] = @[];
                props: JsonNode = newJObject()): uint64 {....raises: [GraphError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc createRelationship(tx: var GraphTx; fromId, toId: uint64; relType: string;
                        props: JsonNode = newJObject()): uint64 {.
    ...raises: [GraphError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc deleteNode(tx: var GraphTx; id: uint64) {....raises: [GraphError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc deleteRelationship(tx: var GraphTx; id: uint64) {....raises: [GraphError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Deletes the relationship with the specified ID 
proc findNodesByLabel(s: GraphStore; label: string): seq[GraphNode] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getNode(s: GraphStore; id: uint64; outNode: var GraphNode): bool {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Retrieves the node with the specified ID. Returns true if found, false otherwise. 
proc getRelationship(s: GraphStore; id: uint64; outRel: var Relationship): bool {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Retrieves the relationship with the specified ID. Returns true if found, false otherwise. 
proc neighbors(s: GraphStore; nodeId: uint64; relType = ""): seq[uint64] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc openGraphStore(rootDir: string): GraphStore {....raises: [OSError, IOError,
    WalError, IOError, GraphError, WalError, OSError, JsonParsingError,
    ValueError, KeyError, Exception], tags: [WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect,
    WriteIOEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Opens or creates a graph store at the specified root directory. The graph store will load any existing snapshot and WAL entries to reconstruct the in-memory graph state.

If the directory does not exist, it will be created. The returned GraphStore object can be used to perform graph operations and transactions.

proc outgoing(s: GraphStore; nodeId: uint64; relType = ""): seq[Relationship] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns the outgoing relationships from the specified node. If relType is provided, only relationships of that type are returned. 
proc rollback(tx: var GraphTx) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc traverseBfs(s: GraphStore; startNode: uint64; maxDepth: int; relType = ""): seq[
    uint64] {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc upsertNode(tx: var GraphTx; node: GraphNode) {....raises: [GraphError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc upsertRelationship(tx: var GraphTx; r: Relationship) {.
    ...raises: [GraphError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Upserts the specified relationship. The relationship must have a valid ID and endpoint node IDs.
